model.label: GPT-5.4
model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: Purdue is the clear pick because the underlying quality gap is enormous: the Boilermakers rank No. 8 in both KenPom and Torvik with a top-two offense nationally, while Queens sits around No. 181 in KenPom and No. 199 in Torvik with a bottom-tier defense. Queens does have a plausible upset path if it turns this into a high-variance shooting game, but its defensive profile gives it very little margin against an elite Purdue attack. I did not find a major availability concern strong enough to materially change that outlook.

reasoningStep.title: Purdue’s elite offense and massive efficiency edge drive the pick
reasoningStep.summary: Purdue owns a huge predictive advantage and faces a Queens team whose defense grades poorly enough to make a 15-over-2 upset highly unlikely absent extreme variance.
evidence: Purdue is No. 8 in both Torvik and KenPom; Queens is No. 199 in Torvik and No. 181 in KenPom.
Purdue’s offense ranks No. 1 in Torvik and No. 2 in KenPom, with adjusted offensive efficiency around 131-133.
Queens’ defense is a major weakness: No. 316 in Torvik defensive rank and 117.3 adjusted defensive efficiency in KenPom.
Recent preview coverage identified Queens as a first-time NCAA tournament team coming from the ASUN automatic bid, while Purdue entered after winning the Big Ten Tournament championship.
Web and ESPN injury/news checks did not surface a major confirmed Purdue availability issue that would justify picking the upset.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.text: Purdue vs Queens preview: March Madness picks, top players, first round game Skip to main content Home Sports Gregg Doyel Colts Pacers Hoosiers Boilermakers Bulldogs Motor Sports Fighting Irish High Schools Indy 500 SportsbookWire Meet the Queens Royals, Purdue basketball's first March Madness opponent Sam King Lafayette Journal & Courier Show Caption Hide Caption Purdue vs Michigan Big Ten Tournament championship expert analysis The Boilermakers avenged a loss to Michigan to win the Big Ten Tournament championship. IndyStar Purdue insider Nathan Baird explains why that's important. Purdue will face Queens in the first round of the NCAA Tournament. This is the first NCAA Tournament appearance for Queens, which transitioned to Division I in 2022. Queens won the A-SUN Tournament to earn an automatic bid into the March Madness field. CHICAGO — Purdue basketball has won seven NCAA Tournament games over the past two March Madness runs. The Boilermakers ' quest for an eighth will be against Queens . Queens earned an automatic bid in the NCAA Tournament field by winning the A-SUN Tournament. It's the first NCAA appearance for the Royals. Here's what you need to know about Purdue basketball's first-round opponent. Purchase NCAA Tournament tickets What to know about Purdue basketball's NCAA Tournament opponent Queens Queens was a Division II program until transitioning to Division I in 2022 and qualified for the NCAA Tournament for the first time this season by defeating West Georgia, Austin Peay and Central Arkansas to win the A-SUN Tournament. The Royals played five games against Power Five programs this season, three of which are NCAA Tournament teams. In those five games, Queens allowed an average of 102.6 points. Villanova and Virginia each scored 94 points, Wake Forest had 111, Arkansas scored 108 and Auburn had 106. Bracket challenge, survivor pool: Free to play, thousands to win! In a loss to Wake Forest, Queens saw former Purdue guard Myles Colvin make 7 of 8 3-pointers and score 33 points. Queens has won seven of its past eight games and avenged a regular-season finale loss by beating ASUN regular-season champion Central Arkansas 98-93 in overtime in the conference tournament championship thanks to 10 3-pointers from Chris Ashby, who scored 34 points. Ashby attended the same high school (Atascocita in Houston) as former Purdue guard Carsen Edwards. Queens advanced to the NCAA Division II Final Four in 2003 and 2018. Queens participated in the CBI Tournament last season, losing to eventual runner-up Cleveland State in the quarterfinals. Queens averages 84.9 points and allows 82.9. The Royals shoot 36% from 3-point range and 48.2% overall. Who are Queens' top players? ∎ Nasir Mann, senior, G (13.4 points, 5.8 rebounds, 2.7 assists, 1.1 steals, ASUN Tournament MVP) ∎ Yoav Berman, sophomore, G (12.6 points, 3.9 rebounds, 3.7 assists, second-team All-ASUN) ∎ Chris Ashby, senior, G (11.7 points, 2.0 rebounds, 1.5 assists, 37.5% 3-pointers) ∎ Avantae P

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
